metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the AI_AGENT population-contract contradiction: either (a) add explicit file-path edit targets and an AC for exporting `AI_AGENT` in the S4/ec5c and S6/7656 shims and move them to Scope IN for this story, or (b) update ec5c and 7656 tickets to include `AI_AGENT` export in their own Scope/AC sections and add a cross-reference here, so the population path has a concrete, owned delivery vehicle.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the capture shims in stories S4 (ec5c) and S6 (7656) are extended by THIS epic to export `AI_AGENT` alongside `REBAR_SESSION_ID`' as the mechanism that closes the DOA gap for `claim_harness`. However, fetching both tickets shows: ec5c (S4) scopes only a SessionStart hook to export `REBAR_SESSION_ID`, with no mention of `AI_AGENT`; 7656 (S6) similarly mentions only `REBAR_SESSION_ID`, with no mention of `AI_AGENT`. The plan's OUT scope says 'the per-harness CAPTURE shims … Claude Code (S4/ec5c) and Codex+Cursor (S6/7656)' while simultaneously claiming those same stories will be 'extended by THIS epic to also export `AI_AGENT`' — a self-contradictory exclusion: if THIS story extends S4/S6, those files are IN scope; if truly OUT, the population contract is ungrounded in any current ticket.
findings.location: What section — 'Harness provenance tag' subsection, population contract paragraph; Scope OUT section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, either move 'AI_AGENT export in S4/S6 shims' to Scope IN (with file targets `scripts/` + per-shim ACs), or add a new sub-bullet under Scope OUT: 'AI_AGENT population requires ec5c (S4) and 7656 (S6) to be updated — file a sub-task or update those tickets' descriptions to include AI_AGENT export in their own ACs before this story is considered complete.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile `CODEX_THREAD_ID` with S6 (7656): S6 states Codex has no native session env var; either remove `CODEX_THREAD_ID` from `_SESSION_ID_VARS` and docs (relying purely on S6's hook-injected `REBAR_SESSION_ID`) or document the discrepancy and confirm with the S6 implementor that Codex has since shipped a native `CODEX_THREAD_ID` env var.
findings.finding: `CODEX_THREAD_ID` is asserted as 'the Codex convention' and added to `_SESSION_ID_VARS`, but the dependent story S6 (7656) — which is the authoritative Codex capture story in this same epic — explicitly states 'Codex explicitly does NOT [have a readable session env var] (no supported programmatic access)'. This is a Contradicted signal: S6 says there is no Codex session env var, yet this plan embeds `CODEX_THREAD_ID` as one in the resolver, docs, schemas, and tests.
findings.location: What section — 'Resolver var-list extension'; `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` `_SESSION_ID_VARS`; AC1 (`pytest … -k codex`); `docs/config.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `CODEX_THREAD_ID` from `_SESSION_ID_VARS` and from `docs/config.md`'s ordered var list. The S6 shim already handles Codex capture by injecting `REBAR_SESSION_ID` via hook; `CODEX_THREAD_ID` is redundant and contradicted. If a native Codex session var is confirmed in the future, it can be added then.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite the actual source for `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` (e.g. OpenCode repo/docs URL + version) and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` (e.g. Codex docs/source); if `CODEX_THREAD_ID` does not exist as a Codex-shipped env var (per S6/7656's own statement), either remove it from the resolver list or document the speculative status explicitly in both the plan and `docs/config.md`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` are the env-var names actually shipped by OpenCode and Codex respectively, citing 'the epic OSS survey' — but no verifiable source (external link, checked source commit, or docs URL) is provided for either name. The companion S6 ticket (7656) further notes that 'Codex explicitly does NOT' have a supported programmatic session env var, which actively contradicts the implicit claim that `CODEX_THREAD_ID` is a real Codex-shipped env var. A wrong name silently produces `None` for every user of that harness with no runtime error, making the feature a silent no-op for those harness users.
findings.location: What section — 'Resolver var-list extension'; Docs section — 'Env-var name provenance'; AC1
findings.suggested_fix: For OpenCode: add a link to the OpenCode source/docs confirming `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` (e.g. `https://github.com/sst/opencode` source grep). For Codex: given S6's explicit statement that Codex has no programmatic session var, either remove `CODEX_THREAD_ID` from `_SESSION_ID_VARS` (and rely on S6's shim to export `REBAR_SESSION_ID` instead), or explicitly label it 'speculative / future-use' in the plan and docs, noting it will silently produce None until/unless Codex adds the var.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite the Scope OUT clause to unambiguously state that shim modification is delegated to a SIBLING story (e.g. S4/ec5c, S6/7656), not this one — remove the parenthetical 'both extended by this epic to also export AI_AGENT' from the OUT bullet or move it to a clarifying note.
findings.finding: The Scope OUT section contains a self-contradictory sentence that would send an implementer in two directions on whether shim modification is in or out of scope for this story.
findings.location: Scope section, OUT clause: 'OUT: the per-harness CAPTURE shims that POPULATE REBAR_SESSION_ID / AI_AGENT — Claude Code (S4 / ec5c) and Codex + Cursor (S6 / 7656), both extended by this epic to also export AI_AGENT.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the OUT bullet to: 'OUT: the per-harness CAPTURE shims that populate REBAR_SESSION_ID / AI_AGENT (Claude Code — S4/ec5c; Codex + Cursor — S6/7656). Those sibling stories are responsible for exporting AI_AGENT; this story only READS and RECORDS it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a single canonical contract table (e.g. in the 'What' section or as a code comment in `txn.py`) that co-locates each write-side event data key with its corresponding read-side state key: `data["harness"] → state["claim_harness"]` and `data["remote_session"] → state["claim_remote_session"]`, so the write/fold pair can never silently diverge.
findings.finding: The event data key names used by the write side to stamp harness/remote-session provenance ('data["harness"]' and 'data["remote_session"]') are defined only in plan prose and never co-located with either the JSON schemas or the reducer fold logic. The plan says 'stamp data["harness"] when present; fold to state["claim_harness"]' and 'stamp data["remote_session"]… fold to state["claim_remote_session"]', but doesn't specify these keys in a single canonical table alongside ticket_state.schema.json or the _fold_claimed_session analog. An implementer writing the reducer fold must infer the exact data key from scattered prose; a mismatch between the write-side key ('data["harness"]') and the read-side key ('data.get("harness")')  silently produces claim_harness=None for every claim.
findings.location: What section — 'Harness provenance tag' paragraph + 'Secondary remote session' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix:
In the 'What' section, add an explicit key-mapping table:

| env var | event data key | state key | compact key |
|---|---|---|---|
| AI_AGENT | data["harness"] | state["claim_harness"] | chn |
| CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID | data["remote_session"] | state["claim_remote_session"] | rsn |

This table should also be reproduced as a comment in `_stamp_session` (or its replacement) in `txn.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the 'What' section whether _stamp_session is extended in-place to also call resolve_harness()/resolve_remote_session() (preferred: single extension point, same two call sites automatically covered) or a new helper is introduced, and name the exact call sites in txn.py that must be updated.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether _stamp_session in txn.py should be EXTENDED to also stamp the two new fields, or whether a new parallel helper (e.g. _stamp_provenance) should be introduced. The plan says 'stamp data["harness"] when present' and 'stamp data["remote_session"]' but never names the stamping function or its call sites. The existing _stamp_session is called in two places (claim_core and transition_core's open->in_progress branch); any new stamping logic must be wired at the same two call sites. An executor who adds a separate call in claim_core but forgets transition_core (or vice versa) produces an inconsistency between claim and bare transition, mirroring the pre-6014 divergence the plan explicitly aims to avoid.
findings.location: What section — 'Harness provenance tag' and 'Secondary remote session' paragraphs; src/rebar/_commands/txn.py lines 56-66 (_stamp_session) and lines 258-259, 428
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Extend `_stamp_session` in `txn.py` to also call `resolve_harness()` and `resolve_remote_session()`, setting `data["harness"]` and `data["remote_session"]` when non-None. This extends the existing single extension point and automatically covers both call sites (claim_core and transition_core).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observable signal (e.g., a DEBUG/INFO log line in `resolve_harness()` when `AI_AGENT` is absent, or a counter increment) so operators can verify harness-provenance capture is working once S4/S6 shims land, and diagnose the gap until then.
findings.finding: The `AI_AGENT` env-var CREATE path is explicitly deferred to S4/S6 shims (marked OUT of scope), meaning `claim_harness` will silently be `None` for every claim during the entire window between this story shipping and those shims landing. No observability mechanism (log warning, metric, doc note) is specified to help operators detect or diagnose this gap.
findings.location: Scope (OUT section) + What ('Population contract' paragraph) + AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: In `resolve_harness()`, emit a structured log at DEBUG level (e.g., `logger.debug('AI_AGENT unset; claim_harness will be None')`) and/or increment a `rebar.claim.harness_missing` counter. Document in `docs/config.md` that `claim_harness` is expected to be `None` until the S4/S6 capture shims are deployed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py` to the 'Existing-test updates' list and extend its hardcoded 3-var clear tuples to all 5 resolver vars (or switch them to import from the updated `_ALL_VARS`).
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py` is an unenumerated consumer of `resolve_session_id()` that hardcodes a 3-var clear list `("REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID")` in two test functions; after the resolver is extended to 5 vars, those tests will not clear `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` or `CODEX_THREAD_ID` from the environment, risking false failures in CI environments that set those vars.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the hardcoded 3-tuples in test_claimed_session_read.py with `from rebar._commands.session_id import _SESSION_ID_VARS` and iterate over that.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py` to the 'Existing-test updates' list and extend its `_SESSION_VARS` tuple to include `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` (or import from `_SESSION_ID_VARS`).
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py` is an unenumerated consumer that hardcodes `_SESSION_VARS = ("REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID")` and uses it in a fixture to clear env vars before every test; after the resolver extension this fixture will not clear the two new vars.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `_SESSION_VARS` at line 24 of test_claimed_session.py with `from rebar._commands.session_id import _SESSION_ID_VARS as _SESSION_VARS`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the Docs section and AC6 to explicitly require updating the inline var-precedence chain in `docs/event-schema.md`'s 'Session provenance' section to the new 5-var form, and add a corresponding assertion to the AC6 doc test (e.g. `assert 'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID' in event_schema_doc`).
findings.finding: The AC6 doc-test assertion checks that `docs/event-schema.md` contains `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session`, but the existing `event-schema.md` 'Session provenance' section already contains the inline var-precedence chain `REBAR_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID -> None` — this will be stale after the resolver is extended to 5 vars. The AC assertion scope does not cover updating this inline chain, so a compliant implementation could pass all ACs while leaving the authoritative session-provenance narrative in `event-schema.md` internally inconsistent with the new 5-var ordered list.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC6; docs/event-schema.md 'Session provenance (claimed_session)' section (lines 125-148)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly declare `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session` as **optional** properties (absent from the `required` array, or typed as nullable with a default) in `ticket_state.schema.json` and `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`, so pre-existing compiled state blobs that lack these keys continue to pass validation without a backfill.
findings.finding: The plan adds `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session` to compiled ticket state but does not explicitly declare these fields as optional/absent-OK in `ticket_state.schema.json`. If the schema marks them as `required`, existing persisted state blobs (compiled before this change) will fail schema validation on read, causing a silent or noisy breakage for all pre-existing tickets.
findings.suggested_fix: In both JSON schemas, add the fields under `properties` with `"type": ["string", "null"]` and a `"default": null`, and do NOT list them in the `required` array. Confirm the read-path deserializer uses `.get("claim_harness")` (or equivalent) rather than a keyed lookup that would raise on absence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback / back-out section asserting the post-rollback schema state: specify that the read path is tolerant of unknown keys (e.g., `additionalProperties` not set to `false` in schemas, deserializer uses `.get()`), so a rollback leaves all records readable without data loss or manual intervention.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the rollback schema state: if this change is deployed and then rolled back, compiled state blobs written during the forward window will contain `claim_harness` / `claim_remote_session` keys that the rolled-back code has never seen. The plan is silent on whether the rolled-back deserializer handles unknown keys gracefully or raises.
findings.location: Scope section; 'State + schema + read surface' subsection

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` against the OpenCode source (e.g. the OpenCode GitHub repo environment or hook docs) before merging; cite the specific file/commit in a code comment on `_SESSION_ID_VARS` so future maintainers can recheck it.
findings.finding: `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` is asserted as a var 'OpenCode ships' and added to `_SESSION_ID_VARS`, config docs, and test labels, but no verifiable external source is cited anywhere in the repo — no URL, no OpenCode source file reference, no survey artifact. Evidence class: Unverified.
findings.location: What section — 'Resolver var-list extension'; `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` `_SESSION_ID_VARS`; AC1 (`pytest … -k opencode`); `docs/config.md` 'Session provenance' paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit deliverable bullet in the What section (and a corresponding Scope IN entry) for creating `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` with test groups covering: `harness`, `remote`, `schema`/`read`, `fork`, and `docs` — matching the -k filters used in AC2–AC6.
findings.finding: The plan references `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` as a proving target across five ACs (AC2–AC6) but never explicitly lists creating this new test file as a deliverable in the What or Scope sections. An executor reading only those sections could miss writing the file, or under-specify its test cases.
findings.location: What section ('Existing-test updates' bullet) and Scope ('IN' list)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to the What section:
**New test file.** Create `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` covering the following pytest -k groups:
- `harness`: `resolve_harness()` returns `AI_AGENT` value; absent `AI_AGENT` → `None`; stamped and folded to `claim_harness`.
- `remote`: `resolve_remote_session()` returns `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID`; folded to `claim_remote_session`.
- `schema`/`read`: both fields default `None` in `make_initial_state`; enumerated in both JSON schemas; present in `TicketStateOut`.
- `fork`: fork-winner gating applies; loser's values are not written; session-less re-claim clears both fields to `None`.
- `docs`: `docs/config.md` contains `AI_AGENT`, `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`, `CODEX_THREAD_ID`; `docs/event-schema.md` contains `claim_harness`, `claim_remote_session`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'Create `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py`' deliverable to the What section (or Scope IN), listing the test cases it must contain: harness provenance, remote session, schema/read surface, fork-winner/clear semantics, and docs assertions.
findings.finding: The new test file `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` is referenced as proof in four ACs but is never named as a deliverable to be created in the What or Scope sections.
findings.location: ACs 2–5 each cite `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py`; What section 'Existing-test updates' bullet describes only updates to `tests/unit/test_session_id.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief `CLAUDE.md` (or equivalent design note) entry capturing the `AI_AGENT` convention: its role as rebar's harness-provenance env var, expected value format, and the population contract shared with S4/ec5c and S6/7656.
findings.finding: The plan establishes `AI_AGENT` as a new rebar-owned cross-component convention (required by the store side here, S4/ec5c, and S6/7656) but only documents it in user-facing `docs/config.md`. No ADR, `CLAUDE.md` update, or design note captures WHY this env var was chosen as the harness-provenance mechanism, what values are expected, and what the population contract is — information that S4 and S6 implementers need to stay aligned.
findings.location: What > Harness provenance tag paragraph; Scope > OUT section; Docs section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to CLAUDE.md such as: '## Harness provenance convention — AI_AGENT is rebar's canonical harness-identity env var. Capture shims (S4/ec5c, S6/7656) MUST export it alongside REBAR_SESSION_ID. Values follow the pattern `<toolname>[_<version>]` (e.g. `claude-code_1.2`, `opencode`, `codex`). The store side (this story) reads it via resolve_harness() and folds to claim_harness; absent = None (no error).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` and `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` to the plan's named edit targets (stamp `data["harness"]`/`data["remote_session"]` in `_stamp_session` or a new parallel helper; fold them in `_fold_claimed_session` or dedicated `_fold_harness`/`_fold_remote_session` functions).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` (contains `_stamp_session`) and `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (contains `_fold_claimed_session`) are the only write-stamp and reducer-fold sites respectively, but neither is listed as an edit target in the plan's What or Scope sections. Both files MUST be changed to stamp `data["harness"]` / `data["remote_session"]` and fold them into state.
findings.location: What section — 'Harness provenance tag' and 'Secondary remote session' subsections; Scope IN
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, under 'Harness provenance tag', add: 'Edit `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` (`_stamp_session`) to also call `resolve_harness()` and `resolve_remote_session()`, stamping `data["harness"]` and `data["remote_session"]` when non-None. Edit `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (`_fold_claimed_session` or a new parallel helper called at the same invocation sites) to fold those keys into `state["claim_harness"]` and `state["claim_remote_session"]`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` (CREATE — new file) explicitly to the Scope IN list and the What section's 'Existing-test updates' bullet.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` is referenced as the proof target in 5 of 6 ACs but does not exist in the repo and is not explicitly listed as a file to CREATE in the plan's What or Scope sections.
findings.location: AC2–AC6 (all cite `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py`); Scope IN ('unit tests')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the 'Harness provenance tag' section explaining WHY harness and remote-session provenance are stamped onto the existing STATUS event's `data` dict (e.g. causal binding to the claim operation, avoids a new event type and `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` update, consistent with the `claimed_session` precedent).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session` are stamped onto the existing STATUS event's `data` dict (piggybacking the claim event) rather than emitted as a separate new event type. G6 requires the plan to articulate why the chosen structural approach fits — its absence leaves implementers without the design intent and makes the plan harder to defend in review.
findings.location: What > Harness provenance tag section; Scope > IN list
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Harness provenance tag' paragraph, add: 'We stamp `harness` and `remote_session` directly onto the existing `open -> in_progress` STATUS event's `data` dict (same pattern as `session`) because harness provenance is causally bound to the claim operation, piggybacks with zero new event types or `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` changes, and is forward/back-compatible by the same additive-key rule already proven for `session`.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
